title: Media, Connectors, Transceivers, and Architectures · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Select and document a campus and data-center physical design under distance, bandwidth, environment, and redundancy constraints. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a physical media and topology bill of design after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, PoE, coaxial, DAC, and twinax use cases.
Compare single-mode and multimode fiber, wavelengths, distance, bandwidth, bend, contamination, and safety concerns.
Compare 802.11, cellular, and satellite media by coverage, latency, capacity, interference, mobility, and cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: network media topologies. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Campus Datacenter Architecture trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic.

content.text: For Net Campus Datacenter Architecture, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Net Campus Datacenter Architecture precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network media topologies. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Spine-leaf eliminates oversubscription and all possible single points of failure.

stimulus.title: Net Campus Datacenter Architecture case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network media topologies. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Spine-leaf eliminates oversubscription and all possible single points of failure.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic. Artifact under review: a complete first version of a physical media and topology bill of design while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Net Campus Datacenter Architecture, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Campus Datacenter Architecture applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic.
